You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.6 (commit: 1ed19c87).

## Issue to Resolve

Consider deprecating or changing the behaviour of CoroutineContext.isActive
According to the [doc](https://kotlin.github.io/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines/is-active.html), `isActive` has the following property:

>The coroutineContext.isActive expression is a shortcut for coroutineContext[Job]?.isActive == true. See [Job.isActive](https://kotlin.github.io/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines/-job/is-active.html).

It means that, if the `Job` is not present, `isActive` always returns `false`.

We have multiple reports that such behaviour can be error-prone when used with non-`kotlinx.coroutines` entry points, such as Ktor and `suspend fun main`, because it is inconsistent with the overall contract:

>(Job) has not been completed and was not cancelled yet


`CoroutineContext.isActive` predates both `CoroutineScope` (which should always have a `Job` in it, if it's not `GlobalScope`)  and `job` extension, so it may be the case that it can be safely deprecated.

Basically, we have three options:

* Do nothing, left things as is. It doesn't solve the original issue, but also doesn't introduce any potentially breaking changes
* Deprecate `CoroutineContext.isActive`. Such change has multiple potential downsides
    * Its only possible replacement is `this.job.isActive`, but this replacement is not equivalent to the original method -- `.job` throws an exception for contexts without a `Job`. An absence of replacement can be too disturbing as [a lot of code](https://grep.app/search?q=context.isActive&filter[lang][0]=Kotlin) rely on a perfectly fine `ctxWithJob.isActive`
    * Code that relies on `.job.isActive` no longer can be called from such entry points safely
* Change the default behaviour -- return `true`. It also "fixes" such patterns as `GlobalScope.isActive` but basically is a breaking change

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

> It also "fixes" such patterns as `GlobalScope.isActive`

`GlobalScope.isActive` is not the same property, and already defaults to `true` when there is no job, so this one is not broken:
https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/master/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/CoroutineScope.kt#L137

This is why I believe it's often more correct to use `CoroutineScope.isActive` rather than `CoroutineContext.isActive`.

> Its only possible replacement is `this.job.isActive`

Why, though? Isn't `this[Job]?.isActive == true` another perfectly valid replacement that doesn't change behaviour? Or is this what you mean by "absence of replacement" - just inlining the property?

I would also be reluctant in suggesting the `.job.isActive` replacement. Very often the authors of those pieces of code just didn't think of the jobless case, and here we would push them towards making their code fail even harder in this case (possibly unknowingly).

> An absence of replacement can be too disturbing as [a lot of code](https://grep.app/search?q=context.isActive&filter%5Blang%5D%5B0%5D=Kotlin) rely on a perfectly fine `ctxWithJob.isActive`

Without better-looking replacement than the body of the current property getter, users could "fix" their code by wrapping the body of their function with `coroutineScope { ... }` and accessing the `CoroutineScope.isActive` property from that scope (which would now have a job). Or just use `yield()` if they want to cancel a non-suspending loop. Or just remove that condition altogether because they didn't need it in the first place (calling other suspend functions with prompt cancellation guarantees already).

Whether we should deprecate `CoroutineContext.isActive` is rather a conceptual matter of whether we want to give meaning to this property when there is no job.

---
TL;DR: I like both the deprecation option and the default change option, but if we deprecate I find the replacement `.job.isActive` harmful. What I like about the change of default is that even though it's technically breaking, it will probably fix more things than break them, and without any change in the ecosystem but version bumps.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/Job.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2016-2021 JetBrains s.r.o. Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 license.
  3â†’ */
  4â†’
  5â†’@file:JvmMultifileClass
  6â†’@file:JvmName("JobKt")
  7â†’@file:Suppress("DEPRECATION_ERROR", "RedundantUnitReturnType")
  8â†’
  9â†’package kotlinx.coroutines
 10â†’
 11â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
 12â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 13â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 14â†’
 15â†’// --------------- core job interfaces ---------------
 16â†’
 17â†’/**
 18â†’ * A background job. Conceptually, a job is a cancellable thing with a life-cycle that
 19â†’ * culminates in its completion.
 20â†’ *
 21â†’ * Jobs can be arranged into parent-child hierarchies where cancellation
 22â†’ * of a parent leads to immediate cancellation of all its [children] recursively.
 23â†’ * Failure of a child with an exception other than [CancellationException] immediately cancels its parent and,
 24â†’ * consequently, all its other children. This behavior can be customized using [SupervisorJob].
 25â†’ *
 26â†’ * The most basic instances of `Job` interface are created like this:
 27â†’ *
 28â†’ * * **Coroutine job** is created with [launch][CoroutineScope.launch] coroutine builder.
 29â†’ *   It runs a specified block of code and completes on completion of this block.
 30â†’ * * **[CompletableJob]** is created with a `Job()` factory function.
 31â†’ *   It is completed by calling [CompletableJob.complete].
 32â†’ *
 33â†’ * Conceptually, an execution of a job does not produce a result value. Jobs are launched solely for their
 34â†’ * side effects. See [Deferred] interface for a job that produces a result.
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ * ### Job states
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * A job has the following states:
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * | **State**                        | [isActive] | [isCompleted] | [isCancelled] |
 41â†’ * | -------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------- | ------------- |
 42â†’ * | _New_ (optional initial state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `false`       |
 43â†’ * | _Active_ (default initial state) | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 44â†’ * | _Completing_ (transient state)   | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 45â†’ * | _Cancelling_ (transient state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `true`        |
 46â†’ * | _Cancelled_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `true`        |
 47â†’ * | _Completed_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `false`       |
 48â†’ *
 49â†’ * Usually, a job is created in the _active_ state (it is created and started). However, coroutine builders
 50â†’ * that provide an optional `start` parameter create a coroutine in the _new_ state when this parameter is set to
 51â†’ * [CoroutineStart.LAZY]. Such a job can be made _active_ by invoking [start] or [join].
 52â†’ *
 53â†’ * A job is _active_ while the coroutine is working or until [CompletableJob] is completed,
 54â†’ * or until it fails or cancelled.
 55â†’ *
 56â†’ * Failure of an _active_ job with an exception makes it _cancelling_.
 57â†’ * A job can be cancelled at any time with [cancel] function that forces it to transition to
 58â†’ * the _cancelling_ state immediately. The job becomes _cancelled_  when it finishes executing its work and
 59â†’ * all its children complete.
 60â†’ *
 61â†’ * Completion of an _active_ coroutine's body or a call to [CompletableJob.complete] transitions the job to
 62â†’ * the _completing_ state. It waits in the _completing_ state for all its children to complete before
 63â†’ * transitioning to the _completed_ state.
 64â†’ * Note that _completing_ state is purely internal to the job. For an outside observer a _completing_ job is still
 65â†’ * active, while internally it is waiting for its children.
 66â†’ *
 67â†’ * ```
 68â†’ *                                       wait children
 69â†’ * +-----+ start  +--------+ complete   +-------------+  finish  +-----------+
 70â†’ * | New | -----> | Active | ---------> | Completing  | -------> | Completed |
 71â†’ * +-----+        +--------+            +-------------+          +-----------+
 72â†’ *                  |  cancel / fail       |
 73â†’ *                  |     +----------------+
 74â†’ *                  |     |
 75â†’ *                  V     V
 76â†’ *              +------------+                           finish  +-----------+
 77â†’ *              | Cancelling | --------------------------------> | Cancelled |
 78â†’ *              +------------+                                   +-----------+
 79â†’ * ```
 80â†’ *
 81â†’ * A `Job` instance in the
 82â†’ * [coroutineContext](https://kotlinlang.org/api/latest/jvm/stdlib/kotlin.coroutines/coroutine-context.html)
 83â†’ * represents the coroutine itself.
 84â†’ *
 85â†’ * ### Cancellation cause
 86â†’ *
 87â†’ * A coroutine job is said to _complete exceptionally_ when its body throws an exception;
 88â†’ * a [CompletableJob] is completed exceptionally by calling [CompletableJob.completeExceptionally].
 89â†’ * An exceptionally completed job is cancelled and the corresponding exception becomes the _cancellation cause_ of the job.
 90â†’ *
 91â†’ * Normal cancellation of a job is distinguished from its failure by the type of this exception that caused its cancellation.
 92â†’ * A coroutine that threw [CancellationException] is considered to be _cancelled normally_.
 93â†’ * If a cancellation cause is a different exception type, then the job is considered to have _failed_.
 94â†’ * When a job has _failed_, then its parent gets cancelled with the exception of the same type,
 95â†’ * thus ensuring transparency in delegating parts of the job to its children.
 96â†’ *
 97â†’ * Note, that [cancel] function on a job only accepts [CancellationException] as a cancellation cause, thus
 98â†’ * calling [cancel] always results in a normal cancellation of a job, which does not lead to cancellation
 99â†’ * of its parent. This way, a parent can [cancel] its own children (cancelling all their children recursively, too)
100â†’ * without cancelling itself.
101â†’ *
102â†’ * ### Concurrency and synchronization
103â†’ *
104â†’ * All functions on this interface and on all interfaces derived from it are **thread-safe** and can
105â†’ * be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without external synchronization.
106â†’ *
107â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance
108â†’ *
109â†’ * **`Job` interface and all its derived interfaces are not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**,
110â†’ * as new methods might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
111â†’ */
112â†’public interface Job : CoroutineContext.Element {
113â†’    /**
114â†’     * Key for [Job] instance in the coroutine context.
115â†’     */
116â†’    public companion object Key : CoroutineContext.Key<Job>
117â†’
118â†’    // ------------ state query ------------
119â†’
120â†’    /**
121â†’     * Returns the parent of the current job if the parent-child relationship
122â†’     * is established or `null` if the job has no parent or was successfully completed.
123â†’     *
124â†’     * Accesses to this property are not idempotent, the property becomes `null` as soon
125â†’     * as the job is transitioned to its final state, whether it is cancelled or completed,
126â†’     * and all job children are completed.
127â†’     *
128â†’     * For a coroutine, its corresponding job completes as soon as the coroutine itself
129â†’     * and all its children are complete.
130â†’     *
131â†’     * @see [Job] state transitions for additional details.
132â†’     */
133â†’    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
134â†’    public val parent: Job?
135â†’
136â†’    /**
137â†’     * Returns `true` when this job is active -- it was already started and has not completed nor was cancelled yet.
138â†’     * The job that is waiting for its [children] to complete is still considered to be active if it
139â†’     * was not cancelled nor failed.
140â†’     *
141â†’     * See [Job] documentation for more details on job states.
142â†’     */
143â†’    public val isActive: Boolean
144â†’
145â†’    /**
146â†’     * Returns `true` when this job has completed for any reason. A job that was cancelled or failed
147â†’     * and has finished its execution is also considered complete. Job becomes complete only after
148â†’     * all its [children] complete.
149â†’     *
150â†’     * See [Job] documentation for more details on job states.
151â†’     */
152â†’    public val isCompleted: Boolean
153â†’
154â†’    /**
155â†’     * Returns `true` if this job was cancelled for any reason, either by explicit invocation of [cancel] or
156â†’     * because it had failed or its child or parent was cancelled.
157â†’     * In the general case, it does not imply that the
158â†’     * job has already [completed][isCompleted], because it may still be finishing whatever it was doing and
159â†’     * waiting for its [children] to complete.
160â†’     *
161â†’     * See [Job] documentation for more details on cancellation and failures.
162â†’     */
163â†’    public val isCancelled: Boolean
164â†’
165â†’    /**
166â†’     * Returns [CancellationException] that signals the completion of this job. This function is
167â†’     * used by [cancellable][suspendCancellableCoroutine] suspending functions. They throw exception
168â†’     * returned by this function when they suspend in the context of this job and this job becomes _complete_.
169â†’     *
170â†’     * This function returns the original [cancel] cause of this job if that `cause` was an instance of
171â†’     * [CancellationException]. Otherwise (if this job was cancelled with a cause of a different type, or
172â†’     * was cancelled without a cause, or had completed normally), an instance of [CancellationException] is
173â†’     * returned. The [CancellationException.cause] of the resulting [CancellationException] references
174â†’     * the original cancellation cause that was passed to [cancel] function.
175â†’     *
176â†’     * This function throws [IllegalStateException] when invoked on a job that is still active.
177â†’     *
178â†’     * @suppress **This an internal API and should not be used from general code.**
179â†’     */
180â†’    @InternalCoroutinesApi
181â†’    public fun getCancellationException(): CancellationException
182â†’
183â†’    // ------------ state update ------------
184â†’
185â†’    /**
186â†’     * Starts coroutine related to this job (if any) if it was not started yet.
187â†’     * The result is `true` if this invocation actually started coroutine or `false`
188â†’     * if it was already started or completed.
189â†’     */
190â†’    public fun start(): Boolean
191â†’
192â†’
193â†’    /**
194â†’     * Cancels this job with an optional cancellation [cause].
195â†’     * A cause can be used to specify an error message or to provide other details on
196â†’     * the cancellation reason for debugging purposes.
197â†’     * See [Job] documentation for full explanation of cancellation machinery.
198â†’     */
199â†’    public fun cancel(cause: CancellationException? = null)
200â†’
201â†’    /**
202â†’     * @suppress This method implements old version of JVM ABI. Use [cancel].
203â†’     */
204â†’    @Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
205â†’    public fun cancel(): Unit = cancel(null)
206â†’
207â†’    /**
208â†’     * @suppress This method has bad semantics when cause is not a [CancellationException]. Use [cancel].
209â†’     */
210â†’    @Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
211â†’    public fun cancel(cause: Throwable? = null): Boolean
212â†’
213â†’    // ------------ parent-child ------------
214â†’
215â†’    /**
216â†’     * Returns a sequence of this job's children.
217â†’     *
218â†’     * A job becomes a child of this job when it is constructed with this job in its
219â†’     * [CoroutineContext] or using an explicit `parent` parameter.
220â†’     *
221â†’     * A parent-child relation has the following effect:
222â†’     *
223â†’     * * Cancellation of parent with [cancel] or its exceptional completion (failure)
224â†’     *   immediately cancels all its children.
225â†’     * * Parent cannot complete until all its children are complete. Parent waits for all its children to
226â†’     *   complete in _completing_ or _cancelling_ state.
227â†’     * * Uncaught exception in a child, by default, cancels parent. This applies even to
228â†’     *   children created with [async][CoroutineScope.async] and other future-like
229â†’     *   coroutine builders, even though their exceptions are caught and are encapsulated in their result.
230â†’     *   This default behavior can be overridden with [SupervisorJob].
231â†’     */
232â†’    public val children: Sequence<Job>
233â†’
234â†’    /**
235â†’     * Attaches child job so that this job becomes its parent and
236â†’     * returns a handle that should be used to detach it.
237â†’     *
238â†’     * A parent-child relation has the following effect:
239â†’     * * Cancellation of parent with [cancel] or its exceptional completion (failure)
240â†’     *   immediately cancels all its children.
241â†’     * * Parent cannot complete until all its children are complete. Parent waits for all its children to
242â†’     *   complete in _completing_ or _cancelling_ states.
243â†’     *
244â†’     * **A child must store the resulting [ChildHandle] and [dispose][DisposableHandle.dispose] the attachment
245â†’     * to its parent on its own completion.**
246â†’     *
247â†’     * Coroutine builders and job factory functions that accept `parent` [CoroutineContext] parameter
248â†’     * lookup a [Job] instance in the parent context and use this function to attach themselves as a child.
249â†’     * They also store a reference to the resulting [ChildHandle] and dispose a handle when they complete.
250â†’     *
251â†’     * @suppress This is an internal API. This method is too error prone for public API.
252â†’     */
253â†’    // ChildJob and ChildHandle are made internal on purpose to further deter 3rd-party impl of Job
254â†’    @InternalCoroutinesApi
255â†’    public fun attachChild(child: ChildJob): ChildHandle
256â†’
257â†’    // ------------ state waiting ------------
258â†’
259â†’    /**
260â†’     * Suspends the coroutine until this job is complete. This invocation resumes normally (without exception)
261â†’     * when the job is complete for any reason and the [Job] of the invoking coroutine is still [active][isActive].
262â†’     * This function also [starts][Job.start] the corresponding coroutine if the [Job] was still in _new_ state.
263â†’     *
264â†’     * Note that the job becomes complete only when all its children are complete.
265â†’     *
266â†’     * This suspending function is cancellable and **always** checks for a cancellation of the invoking coroutine's Job.
267â†’     * If the [Job] of the invoking coroutine is cancelled or completed when this
268â†’     * suspending function is invoked or while it is suspended, this function
269â†’     * throws [CancellationException].
270â†’     *
271â†’     * In particular, it means that a parent coroutine invoking `join` on a child coroutine throws
272â†’     * [CancellationException] if the child had failed, since a failure of a child coroutine cancels parent by default,
273â†’     * unless the child was launched from within [supervisorScope].
274â†’     *
275â†’     * This function can be used in [select] invocation with [onJoin] clause.
276â†’     * Use [isCompleted] to check for a completion of this job without waiting.
277â†’     *
278â†’     * There is [cancelAndJoin] function that combines an invocation of [cancel] and `join`.
279â†’     */
280â†’    public suspend fun join()
281â†’
282â†’    /**
283â†’     * Clause for [select] expression of [join] suspending function that selects when the job is complete.
284â†’     * This clause never fails, even if the job completes exceptionally.
285â†’     */
286â†’    public val onJoin: SelectClause0
287â†’
288â†’    // ------------ low-level state-notification ------------
289â†’
290â†’    /**
291â†’     * Registers handler that is **synchronously** invoked once on completion of this job.
292â†’     * When the job is already complete, then the handler is immediately invoked
293â†’     * with the job's exception or cancellation cause or `null`. Otherwise, the handler will be invoked once when this
294â†’     * job is complete.
295â†’     *
296â†’     * The meaning of `cause` that is passed to the handler:
297â†’     * * Cause is `null` when the job has completed normally.
298â†’     * * Cause is an instance of [CancellationException] when the job was cancelled _normally_.
299â†’     *   **It should not be treated as an error**. In particular, it should not be reported to error logs.
300â†’     * * Otherwise, the job had _failed_.
301â†’     *
302â†’     * The resulting [DisposableHandle] can be used to [dispose][DisposableHandle.dispose] the

 11â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
 12â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 13â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 14â†’
 15â†’// --------------- core job interfaces ---------------
 16â†’
 17â†’/**
 18â†’ * A background job. Conceptually, a job is a cancellable thing with a life-cycle that
 19â†’ * culminates in its completion.
 20â†’ *
 21â†’ * Jobs can be arranged into parent-child hierarchies where cancellation
 22â†’ * of a parent leads to immediate cancellation of all its [children] recursively.
 23â†’ * Failure of a child with an exception other than [CancellationException] immediately cancels its parent and,
 24â†’ * consequently, all its other children. This behavior can be customized using [SupervisorJob].
 25â†’ *
 26â†’ * The most basic instances of `Job` interface are created like this:
 27â†’ *
 28â†’ * * **Coroutine job** is created with [launch][CoroutineScope.launch] coroutine builder.
 29â†’ *   It runs a specified block of code and completes on completion of this block.
 30â†’ * * **[CompletableJob]** is created with a `Job()` factory function.
 31â†’ *   It is completed by calling [CompletableJob.complete].
 32â†’ *
 33â†’ * Conceptually, an execution of a job does not produce a result value. Jobs are launched solely for their
 34â†’ * side effects. See [Deferred] interface for a job that produces a result.
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ * ### Job states
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * A job has the following states:
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * | **State**                        | [isActive] | [isCompleted] | [isCancelled] |
 41â†’ * | -------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------- | ------------- |
 42â†’ * | _New_ (optional initial state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `false`       |
 43â†’ * | _Active_ (default initial state) | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 44â†’ * | _Completing_ (transient state)   | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 45â†’ * | _Cancelling_ (transient state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `true`        |
 46â†’ * | _Cancelled_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `true`        |
 47â†’ * | _Completed_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `false`       |
 48â†’ *
 49â†’ * Usually, a job is created in the _active_ state (it is created and started). However, coroutine builders
 50â†’ * that provide an optional `start` parameter create a coroutine in the _new_ state when this parameter is set to
 51â†’ * [CoroutineStart.LAZY]. Such a job can be made _active_ by invoking [start] or [join].
 52â†’ *
 53â†’ * A job is _active_ while the coroutine is working or until [CompletableJob] is completed,
 54â†’ * or until it fails or cancelled.
 55â†’ *
 56â†’ * Failure of an _active_ job with an exception makes it _cancelling_.
 57â†’ * A job can be cancelled at any time with [cancel] function that forces it to transition to
 58â†’ * the _cancelling_ state immediately. The job becomes _cancelled_  when it finishes executing its work and
 59â†’ * all its children complete.
 60â†’ *
 61â†’ * Completion of an _active_ coroutine's body or a call to [CompletableJob.complete] transitions the job to
 62â†’ * the _completing_ state. It waits in the _completing_ state for all its children to complete before
 63â†’ * transitioning to the _completed_ state.
 64â†’ * Note that _completing_ state is purely internal to the job. For an outside observer a _completing_ job is still
 65â†’ * active, while internally it is waiting for its children.
 66â†’ *
 67â†’ * ```
 68â†’ *                                       wait children
 69â†’ * +-----+ start  +--------+ complete   +-------------+  finish  +-----------+
 70â†’ * | New | -----> | Active | ---------> | Completing  | -------> | Completed |
 71â†’ * +-----+        +--------+            +-------------+          +-----------+
 72â†’ *                  |  cancel / fail       |

 86â†’ *
 87â†’ * A coroutine job is said to _complete exceptionally_ when its body throws an exception;
 88â†’ * a [CompletableJob] is completed exceptionally by calling [CompletableJob.completeExceptionally].

100â†’ * without cancelling itself.

103â†’ *
104â†’ * All functions on this interface and on all interfaces derived from it are **thread-safe** and can
105â†’ * be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without external synchronization.

107â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance

110â†’ * as new methods might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
